compiled_state.title: Polyglot Kudos Triggers: language-agnostic T7/T9/T12/T13/T4 replacements
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
The existing kudos system (Epics 3e9b-afee and 71df-5bd8) includes 5 triggers that silently skip on non-Python stacks. T7 uses Python ast.NodeVisitor. T9 uses ruff (Python-only). T12 uses Python-anchored regexes. T13 and T4 use Python ast.dump() subtree hashing. Ruby and JavaScript developers receive zero kudos from these 5 triggers. This epic replaces them with polyglot equivalents so kudos fires across all supported stacks.

## Success Criteria
- T7 uses lizard -ENS instead of Python ast.NodeVisitor; supports Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java; 30s timeout; --exclude for test dirs; when lizard absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal
- T9 reads commands.lint (from Epic B); when absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED; when present, fires on zero-violation result
- T12 uses line-start-anchored regexes covering Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java syntax for commented-out code detection
- T13 and T4 use jscpd (JSON reporter to temp dir, 30s timeout, exit code parsing); when jscpd absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED
- All five triggers emit machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal conforming to the signal format established by Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8); no shared library imported from Kudos Epic 2
- Validation: sprint acceptance test environment has lizard and jscpd installed; test matrix covers all 5 triggers before/after on non-Python files; before: no kudos for Ruby file; after: kudos fires for quality improvement; Kudos Epic 1 must be present in CI or dispatch_kudos_trigger() mocked

## Dependencies
- Epic B (3818-310d): T9 reads commands.lint; without Epic B, T9 always emits TRIGGER_SKIPPED
- Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee): T13 and T7 call the kudos dispatch function delivered by Kudos Epic 1; runtime blocking dependency
- Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8): runtime consumer dependency — Epic C conforms to TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format so the existing event emitter can handle it

## Approach
Replace each of the 5 Python-specific trigger implementations with polyglot equivalents: lizard for AST-based nesting analysis (T7), jscpd for duplicate block detection (T13/T4), language-neutral regexes for commented-out code (T12), and commands.lint config key for lint-clean detection (T9). All triggers emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED when required tooling or config is absent.

comments.body: Runtime dependency on Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee) is captured transitively via the existing C → Kudos2 (71df-5bd8) → Kudos1 (3e9b-afee) chain. A direct link was attempted but correctly blocked by the cycle detector as a redundant transitive edge.
comments.author: Claude
